page: Microsoft Azure E2ads_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by a single physical core of an AMD EPYC 7763 processor operating at 3.525 GHz. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Storage is bundled with a 77 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks reveal a strong single-core CPU capability, with stress-ng single-core performance ranking in the top 25%. However, multi-core and parallel processing metrics are limited by the 2-vCPU configuration, resulting in weak multi-core CPU performance and a poor rating for software compilation. Memory bandwidth tests show weak to poor performance, with read-only large block bandwidth at 34.27 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 22.5 GB/sec. Text processing and single-threaded compression tasks perform at average levels.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E2ads_v5 offers a high memory density per vCPU, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing power. The primary tradeoff is the low multi-core performance and poor database operations throughput. This server is suited for lightweight memory-intensive applications, small-scale caching, single-threaded data processing, and basic web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E2ads_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. Built on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance includes a 77 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth are relatively weak. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides high memory density, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound tasks that do not demand heavy parallel processing. It is suited for lightweight caching, single-threaded data processing, and small-scale memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM density, dedicated AMD EPYC processing, and local storage for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
Features 16.0 GB of RAM providing a high 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio
Includes 77 GB of local storage and Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Best suited for lightweight caching and memory-bound workloads
